data.title: Ares Management raises over $2.3B to support Aspida’s growth
data.description:
Ares Management (NYSE:ARES) has secured over $2.3B of equity commitments to support the long-term growth of Aspida Holdings.

Launched four years ago, Aspida is a life insurance and annuity company focused on providing retirement and reinsurance solutions, with total assets of over $19B as of Sept. 30, 2024.

The Durham, N.C.-based company is supported by the Ares Insurance Solutions business as its dedicated investment management, capital solutions and corporate development partner.

The company recently closed an oversubscribed offering of $700M in credit facilities, bringing total capital raised to more than $3B.

data.title: Volkswagen sees deliveries fall in 2024 amid weakness in China
data.description:
Volkswagen (OTCPK:VLKAF) announced on Thursday that it delivered around 4.8 million vehicles globally in 2024, despite headwinds such as sluggish economic activity, political challenges, and intense competition in regions like China. The deliveries tally was down 1.4% from the prior year.

The company highlighted that it was the leader in its home nation, with 19.1% market share, according to the German Federal Motor Transport Authority. The top ten most popular vehicles in Germany included as many as five Volkswagen models (the Golf, T-Roc, Tiguan, Passat and Polo). Volkswagen (OTCPK:VLKAF) also topped out the German market in the BEV segment.

Globally, deliveries in Europe were down 1.7% Y/Y to 1,254,500 and deliveries in China fell 8.3% to 2,198,900, Deliveries in South America rose 21.1% to 479,400 and were up 18.4% in North America to 592,300.

Volkswagen (OTCPK:VLKAF) noted that SUVs were in high demand in 2024, both with conventional drive systems and as all-electric variants. SUVs now account for 47% of total Volkswagen deliveries – an increase of 1.4 percentage points on 2023. Once again, the Tiguan was the most popular SUV and best-selling model, followed by the T-Roc.

"We are beginning the new year with optimism. Not only do we have attractive products, but we are meeting the needs of a wide variety of customer groups with our extensive, diverse model range that covers all drive types," stated Volkswagen Board Member for Sales, Marketing and After Sales Martin Sander.

data.title: Apogee raises dividend by 4%
data.description:
• Apogee (NASDAQ:APOG) declared $0.26/share quarterly dividend, 4% increase from prior dividend of $0.25.
  • Forward yield 1.98%
  • Payable Feb. 12; for shareholders of record Jan. 28; ex-div Jan. 28.

data.title: Japan's Epson collaborates Kitchen Armor for kitchen solutions
data.description:
• Japna's Epson, led by Seiko Japan (OTCPK:SEKEY) partnered with Kitchen Armor, a restaurant kitchen solutions provider offering a full range of standard and custom kitchen automation products.
  • Through this partnership, Epson and Kitchen Armor are providing easy-to-implement kitchen technology to mid-sized chains and single location operators.

  • "Many restaurants are looking to optimize kitchen efficiency, especially when managing orders coming from a range of third-party ordering and delivery apps, and we are excited to work with Kitchen Armor to deliver world-class solutions to meet these needs." said said Tessa Kohl, product manager, Epson America.

data.title: Royal Bank of Canada to partner with Cohere for deployment of AI solution
data.description:
• Royal Bank of Canada (NYSE:RY) (TSX:RY:CA) announced on Thursday a partnership with the AI firm Cohere for the deployment of an enterprise generative AI solution.
  • The platform, called North for Banking, will integrate with RBC's and Cohere's foundation models.
  • North for Banking is also set to integrate with the Canadian bank's internal platforms.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Apple clarifies Siri's privacy features after $95M lawsuit settlement
data.description:
Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) said on Wednesday that it has never used Siri data to build marketing profiles, never made it available for advertising, and never sold it to anyone for any purpose.

The clarification comes after last week the U.S. tech giant agreed to pay $95M to settle a lawsuit which alleged that voice assistant Siri violated users' privacy.

Apple noted that is constantly developing technologies to make Siri even more private, and will continue to do so.

The company noted that to protect user privacy, Siri is designed to do as much processing as possible on a user's device, allowing for personalized experiences without having to transfer and analyze personal information on Apple servers.

"Although Apple attempts to do as much as possible on device, certain features require real-time input from Apple servers. And when that's the case, Siri uses as little data as possible to deliver an accurate result," the company said in a press release.

The company added that Siri searches and requests are not associated with the user's Apple Account. A random identifier — a long string of letters and numbers associated with a single device — is used to keep track of data while it is being processed, rather than tying it to a user's identity through their Apple Account or phone number. Apple said they believe this process is unique among digital assistants in use today.

In addition, Apple said that it does not retain audio recordings of Siri interactions unless users opt in to help improve Siri, and even then, the recordings are used solely for that purpose. Users can opt out at any time, according to the company.

Apple also provided information on the use of Siri with its AI tool Apple Intelligence.

The company said that to protect users' privacy at every step, many of the models that power Apple Intelligence run entirely on the device.

For Apple Intelligence requests which require access to larger models, Private Cloud Compute extends the privacy and security of iPhone into the cloud. When Siri uses Private Cloud Compute, a user's data is not stored or made accessible to Apple, and Private Cloud Compute only uses their data to fulfill the request, the company added.

data.title: Clear Channel Outdoor to divest Europe-North segment for $625M
data.description:
Clear Channel Outdoor (NYSE:CCO) is selling its Europe-North segment to a subsidiary of Bauer Media Group in a $625M all-cash deal.

The all-cash consideration represents a transaction multiple of ~6.5x Europe-North segment results for the twelve months ended Sept. 30, 2024. The transaction is expected to close in 2025, upon satisfaction of regulatory approvals.

"This agreement to sell our Europe-North segment is another significant step in the execution of our strategic plan to optimize our portfolio and focus on growing our America and Airports segments to organically improve cash flow and reduce leverage on our balance sheet," said CEO Scott Wells.

Anticipated net proceeds from the sale will be used by the company to prepay in full the outstanding CCIBV term loans in the principal amount of $375M, plus any accrued interest. The remaining proceeds will be subject to the asset sale provisions of the agreements governing the remainder of the company's indebtedness.

data.title: Commodity Roundup: Copper futures extend rally; China’s lithium reserves world’s second-largest
data.description:
Copper futures (HG1:COM) extended their winning streak +1.33% to a fourth trading session on Thursday, as investors continued to weigh the outlook for China, while the country also became the world's second-largest holder of lithium reserves.

The consumer price index rose 0.1% in December from a year earlier, while factory deflation extended into a 27th month, though the producer price index recorded a slower drop of 2.3%, the National Bureau of Statistics said Thursday.

However, some market participants remain optimistic that China will follow through on its recent commitments to ramp up monetary and fiscal stimulus to support economic growth.

Meanwhile, China’s lithium reserves have risen from 6% to 16.5% of the global total, making it the world’s second-largest holder of lithium reserves, state media reported on Wednesday.

The newly discovered mines include a 2 800-km-long spodumene mine in the Xikunsong-Pan-Ganzi region in Tibet, and some lithium salt lakes in the Qinghai-Tibet Plateau, Xinhua News Agency said in the report.

Potentially relevant tickers include (ALB), (SQM), (LAC), (LAAC), (ALTM), (PLL), (SLI), (OTCPK:PILBF)

ETF: (NYSEARCA:LIT), (BATT)

Turning to the physical gold market in the second-biggest buyer, India has slashed its November gold import estimates by an unprecedented $5 billion, the largest revision for any commodity in history, after errors in preliminary calculations inflated the figure to a record, Reuters reported.

New Delhi reported last month that its gold imports surged to a record high of $14.8 billion in November, more than doubling from $7.13 billion in October.

Elsewhere, Federal Reserve officials indicated that the central bank will have to take a “careful approach” in cutting interest rates further due to the rising risk that inflation will remain persistently higher than its 2% target.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Should stock investors be afraid of rising Treasury yields?
data.description:
The stock market is closed Thursday for former President Jimmy Carter's state funeral, but bond markets are open until 2 PM ET. Interesting things have been happening in the fixed-income world over the past few months as the Fed embarked on an easing cycle. All three interest rate cuts by the central bank since September have seen a subsequent INCREASE in longer-term Treasury yields (though those are more associated with the longer-term monetary and fiscal landscape, as well as economic conditions).

Snapshot: The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose to 4.73% on Wednesday, a whole percentage point higher than the level seen only a quarter ago, while the 20-year Treasury (US20Y) topped 5% for the first time since 2023. Concerns over the national debt and debt ceiling are at play, along with possible inflationary threats like higher tariffs, tax cuts, and mass deportations. Worries over fiscal discipline are also not restricted to the U.S., with borrowing costs in France now above those of Greece, while gilt yields in the U.K. have been pushed to their highest levels since 2008.

Investors are demanding larger premiums in response to the uncertainty, but there are also risks that this can turn into bond market activism or even so-called "bond vigilantism." In the current environment, central banks have stepped back from their role as a dominant force in bond markets, which was conducted via quantitative easing following the financial crisis. That has given additional power to fund managers and other market players, leaving many wondering what it might mean for equity markets.

SA commentary: "While stocks may struggle during periods when long-term rates are rising, that struggle should be temporary so long as the increase is for good reason," writes SA Investing Group Leader Lawrence Fuller. "As we have become accustomed in the past two years, the pessimists and fearmongers are warning of doom and gloom ahead, but markets are simply adjusting to the normalization of the yield curve, as the economic expansion continues." Read more in Rates Are Rising For The Right Reason, So Don't Panic

More on bonds

  • Bond Market Massacre: Could Surging Rates End The Epic Bull Market For Stocks?
  • US10Y soars about 100 bps since Fed rate cuts, signaling a diverging inflation outlook
  • SocGen sees sign of bonds becoming more attractive to hold vs. stocks
  • Yellen decodes recent yield climb, pointing to term premium, rate repricing

data.title: Vanguard Group raises stake in Talen Energy
data.description:
Vanguard Group has raised its stake in Talen Energy (NASDAQ:TLN) to 10.41%, a regulatory filing showed on Wednesday.

The investment manager purchased over 4M shares of the utility's common stock, representing a 10.41% interest. It held about 9.9% of its outstanding shares as of October.

The additional investment comes as Big Tech firms turn to utilities to power their AI data centers.

Shares of Talen Energy (TLN) have jumped around 240% over the past year and are rated as “Strong Buy” by Wall Street analysts.
